title: Coffee-Shop Breach — black hat vs white hat vs red hat
summary: An attacker breaches a café point-of-sale on open WiFi, the SOC detects and contains it, and the red team closes the hole — three actors, one choreographed timeline.
purpose: Shows a full attack-detect-respond-harden loop the way a real range exercise plays out: the same event seen from three perspectives at once. It demonstrates why detection and response matter — the attacker succeeds, then is contained, then permanently locked out after hardening.

steps.say: On the open café WiFi. Inventory the clients (10.95.0.0/24).
out: Nmap scan report for 10.95.0.1  (cafe-gw)     Host is up (0.0011s).
Nmap scan report for 10.95.0.20 (pos-till)    Host is up (0.0009s).
Nmap scan report for 10.95.0.31 (guest-phone) Host is up (0.0024s).
Nmap done: 256 addresses (3 hosts up) scanned in 2.14s

steps.say: There's a POS till host. Fingerprint its services.
out: Nmap done: 1 host up scanned in 6.02s

steps.say: SOC side: the sweep just lit up Wazuh. Watch the alerts.
Try the POS admin panel with default creds.
Confirmed intrusion. Isolate the POS and block the attacker.

steps.run: sudo nft add rule inet filter forward ip saddr 10.95.0.10 drop
out: §[+] drop rule installed — 10.95.0.10 severed from the till segment§

steps.say: Red hat: close the hole for good. Lock the POS to the till VLAN only.
steps.run: echo 'baseline: default-deny inbound, allow only till-VLAN → POS:8080/TLS'
out: baseline: default-deny inbound, allow only till-VLAN → POS:8080/TLS
§[+] pos-firewall.nft loaded — 1 accept, 42 drop rules active§
rotated POS admin credential; default 'admin' account disabled

steps.run: curl -s -m5 http://10.95.0.20:8080/login || echo 'blocked — no route to POS'
out: curl: (28) Connection timed out after 5001 ms

steps.say: Attacker contained, POS hardened. All three timelines captured.
